hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Used when an action will be completed before another in the future.  
Markers: *by, by the time, before*.  
Example: *By next week, I will have finished the project.*

a1.will have tidied up:
✅ Future Perfect – the action will be completed before the guests arrive.  
Formula: **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By the evening, she will have tidied up the room.*
a1.will be tidying up:
❌ Future Continuous – the action in progress, not the result.  
Example: *At 5 pm, she will be tidying up.*
a1.will have been tidying up:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration, not suitable here.  
Example: *By the time you arrive, she will have been tidying up for an hour.*
a1.will tidy up:
❌ Future Simple – just a plan, without the connection of 'earlier/later'.  
Example: *She will tidy up tomorrow.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect Continuous = **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Markers: *for, since, by the end of…*.  
Example: *By the end of 2025, I will have been studying English for 10 years.*

a1.will you have been working:
✅ Future Perfect Continuous – duration up to a future moment.  
Formula: **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *How long will you have been working here by 2030?*
a1.will you be working:
❌ Future Continuous – process at the moment, without duration.  
Example: *Will you be working at 5 pm tomorrow?*
a1.will you have worked:
❌ Future Perfect – result, not duration.  
Example: *By next year, you will have worked here for 2 years.*
a1.will you work:
❌ Future Simple – general fact in the future.  
Example: *Will you work here next year?*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Markers: *by 10 o’clock, by tomorrow*.  
Example: *By the end of the day, she will have finished the report.*

a1.will have done:
✅ Future Perfect – the action will be completed by 10 o’clock.  
Formula: **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By 9 pm, I will have done my homework.*
a1.will have been doing:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – emphasizes the process, not the result.  
Example: *By 10 pm, I will have been doing this task for two hours.*
a1.will do:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *I will do it tomorrow.*
a1.will be doing:
❌ Future Continuous – process at the moment.  
Example: *At 10 pm, I will be doing my homework.*

hints:
{a1} Future Simple = **will + V1**.  
Used for simple future actions.  
Example: *I will help you tomorrow.*

a1.will tell:
✅ Future Simple – simple action in the future.  
Formula: **will + V1**.  
Example: *She will tell me the truth.*
a1.will be telling:
❌ Future Continuous – process.  
Example: *At 5 pm, she will be telling a story.*
a1.will have told:
❌ Future Perfect – result in the future.  
Example: *By 6 pm, she will have told the news.*
a1.will have been telling:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – action in duration.  
Example: *By 6 pm, she will have been telling stories for an hour.*

hints:
{a1} Future Continuous = **will + be + V-ing**.  
Markers: *this time tomorrow, at 5 pm tomorrow*.  
Example: *This time tomorrow, I will be working.*

a1.will be watching:
✅ Future Continuous – action in progress at a specific time.  
Formula: **will + be + V-ing**.  
Example: *At 8 pm tomorrow, I will be watching TV.*
a1.will have watched:
❌ Future Perfect – completed action.  
Example: *By 9 pm, I will have watched two films.*
a1.will have been watching:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – action in duration.  
Example: *By 9 pm, I will have been watching TV for 2 hours.*
a1.will watch:
❌ Future Simple – plan.  
Example: *I will watch TV tomorrow.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Markers: *by, before*.  
Example: *By 2025, I will have known him for 10 years.*

a1.will have known:
✅ Future Perfect – result by a certain moment.  
Formula: **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By next year, I will have known her for a year.*
a1.will have been knowing: ❌ Incorrect form – the verb *know* is not used in Continuous.
a1.will know:
❌ Future Simple – general fact, no emphasis on completion.  
Example: *I will know her tomorrow.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect Continuous = **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Markers: *for two hours, by noon, since morning*.  
Example: *By the evening, I will have been studying for 6 hours.*

a1.will have been working:
✅ Future Perfect Continuous – action lasts for a certain time until the moment.  
Formula: **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *By noon, he will have been working for 5 hours.*
a1.will have worked:
❌ Future Perfect – emphasis on completion, not on duration.  
Example: *By noon, he will have worked here for 5 years.*
a1.will work:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *He will work tomorrow.*
a1.will be working:
❌ Future Continuous – process at a certain moment.  
Example: *At 10 am, he will be working.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By tomorrow, I will have read this article.*

a1.will have read:
✅ Future Perfect – action will be completed in the future (three times).  
Formula: **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By next month, I will have read this book twice.*
a1.will have been reading:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – emphasizes duration.  
Example: *By evening, I will have been reading for 2 hours.*
a1.will be reading:
❌ Future Continuous – process.  
Example: *Tomorrow evening, I will be reading.*
a1.will read:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *I will read this book tomorrow.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By the time he arrives, I will have finished my work.*

a1.will have used up:
✅ Future Perfect – by a certain moment everything will be used.  
Formula: **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By 2050, people will have used up most of the resources.*
a1.will be using:
❌ Future Continuous – process.  
Example: *At 5 pm, they will be using oil.*
a1.will use:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *People will use more energy in the future.*
a1.will have been using:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration, not completion.  
Example: *By 2050, people will have been using oil for 200 years.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect Continuous = **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *By 2026, I will have been studying for 10 years.*

a1.will have been studying:
✅ Future Perfect Continuous – action will last six years.  
Formula: **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *By next year, I will have been studying English for 7 years.*
a1.will study:
❌ Future Simple – plan.  
Example: *I will study English tomorrow.*
a1.will be studying:
❌ Future Continuous – process.  
Example: *At 5 pm, I will be studying.*
a1.will have studied:
❌ Future Perfect – result, not duration.  
Example: *By June, I will have studied here for 2 years.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By 2025, will you have finished school?*

a1.Will you have paid:
✅ Future Perfect – completion by a certain age.  
Formula: **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *Will you have paid the debt by 2030?*
a1.Will you pay:
❌ Future Simple – general question about the future.  
Example: *Will you pay for it tomorrow?*
a1.Will you be paying:
❌ Future Continuous – process.  
Example: *Will you be paying at 5 pm?*
a1.Will you have been paying:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration.  
Example: *By 2030, will you have been paying for 20 years?*

hints:
{a1} Future Continuous = **will + be + V-ing**.  
Markers: *this time tomorrow*.  
Example: *Tomorrow at 5 pm, I will be flying.*

a1.will be flying:
✅ Future Continuous – process at a certain moment.  
Formula: **will + be + V-ing**.  
Example: *This time tomorrow, I will be flying over the ocean.*
a1.will have been flying:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration of the flight.  
Example: *By 6 pm, I will have been flying for 2 hours.*
a1.will fly:
❌ Future Simple – plan.  
Example: *I will fly to Paris next week.*
a1.will have flown:
❌ Future Perfect – result.  
Example: *By 6 pm, I will have flown 500 miles.*

hints:
{a1} Future Simple = **will + V1**.  
Example: *I will try again tomorrow.*

a1.will try:
✅ Future Simple – simple decision in the future.  
Formula: **will + V1**.  
Example: *I will try to help him tomorrow.*
a1.will be trying:
❌ Future Continuous – process.  
Example: *At 5 pm, I will be trying to call him.*
a1.will have tried:
❌ Future Perfect – completed action.  
Example: *By tomorrow, I will have tried to fix it.*
a1.will have been trying:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration.  
Example: *By evening, I will have been trying for hours.*

hints:
{a1} Future Continuous = **will + be + V-ing**.  
Example: *This time tomorrow, I will be working.*

a1.will be working:
✅ Future Continuous – action in progress.  
Formula: **will + be + V-ing**.  
Example: *Next week, he will be working overtime.*
a1.will work:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *He will work next week.*
a1.will have worked:
❌ Future Perfect – result.  
Example: *By Friday, he will have worked 40 hours.*
a1.will have been working:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration.  
Example: *By Friday, he will have been working all week.*

hints:
{a1} Future Continuous = **will + be + V-ing**.  
Example: *Tomorrow at 3 pm, I will be having a lesson.*

a1.will be having:
✅ Future Continuous – process at a specific time.  
Formula: **will + be + V-ing**.  
Example: *At 3 pm, I will be having a meeting.*
a1.will have:
❌ Future Simple – fact.  
Example: *I will have a meeting tomorrow.*
a1.will have been having:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration.  
Example: *By 4 pm, I will have been having a meeting for an hour.*
a1.will have had:
❌ Future Perfect – result.  
Example: *By 4 pm, I will have had a meeting.*

hints:
{a1} Future Continuous = **will + be + V-ing**.  
Example: *In 20 years, we will be using new sources of energy.*

a1.will be using:
✅ Future Continuous – the process of using.  
Formula: **will + be + V-ing**.  
Example: *By 2060, people will be using new technologies.*
a1.will have used:
❌ Future Perfect – completion, not a process.  
Example: *By 2060, people will have used all oil.*
a1.will have been using:
❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration.  
Example: *By 2060, they will have been using solar power for decades.*
a1.will use:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *In the future, people will use more solar energy.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect Continuous = **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *By evening, we will have been playing all day.*

a1.will have been playing:
✅ Future Perfect Continuous – the action will last all day.  
Formula: **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *By evening, I will have been playing football for 5 hours.*
a1.will be playing:
❌ Future Continuous – process at the moment.  
Example: *At 5 pm, I will be playing football.*
a1.will have played:
❌ Future Perfect – completion.  
Example: *By 5 pm, I will have played two matches.*
a1.will play:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *I will play football tomorrow.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect Continuous = **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *By tomorrow, I will have been working for 5 hours.*

a1.will have been doing:
✅ Future Perfect Continuous – the action will last an hour.  
Formula: **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *By 11 am, I will have been doing the exam for an hour.*
a1.will have done:
❌ Future Perfect – completion.  
Example: *By 11 am, I will have done the exam.*
a1.will do:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *I will do the exam tomorrow.*
a1.will be doing:
❌ Future Continuous – process at the moment.  
Example: *At 11 am, I will be doing the exam.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By 8 pm, she will have left home.*

a1.will have left:
✅ Future Perfect – the action will be completed by 1:30.  
Formula: **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By 2 pm, he will have left the office.*
a1.will leave:
❌ Future Simple – fact.  
Example: *He will leave at 2 pm.*
a1.will be leaving:
❌ Future Continuous – process.  
Example: *At 1.30, he will be leaving.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will (not) + have + V3**.  
Example: *By the deadline, I won’t have finished the task.*

a1.won’t have finished:
✅ Future Perfect (negative) – the action will not be completed.  
Formula: **will not + have + V3**.  
Example: *I won’t have finished the report by 5 pm.*
a1.won’t be finishing:
❌ Future Continuous (negative) – process.  
Example: *At 5 pm, I won’t be finishing the report.*
a1.won’t finish:
❌ Future Simple (negative) – just a fact.  
Example: *I won’t finish it tomorrow.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By next week, I will have woken up earlier every day.*
{a2} Future Perfect = **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By the time you come, I will have opened the book.*

a1.will have woken up:
✅ Future Perfect – the action will be completed.  
Formula: **will + have + V3**.  
Example: *By 8 am, he will have woken up.*

a2.will have opened:
✅ Future Perfect – the gifts have already been opened.  
Example: *By the time you arrive, I will have opened the gift.*
a2.will have been opening: ❌ Future Perfect Continuous – duration, not needed here.
a2.will be opening:
❌ Future Continuous – process.  
Example: *At 9 am, he will be opening his presents.*

hints:
{a1} Future Perfect Continuous = **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *By the time you come, he will have been eating for an hour.*

a1.will have been eating:
✅ Future Perfect Continuous – the action lasted for a certain time.  
Formula: **will + have + been + V-ing**.  
Example: *He will have been eating for an hour when she arrives.*
a1.will have eaten:
❌ Future Perfect – completion, not duration.  
Example: *By 6 pm, he will have eaten dinner.*
a1.will eat:
❌ Future Simple – general fact.  
Example: *He will eat sweets tomorrow.*
a1.will be eating:
❌ Future Continuous – process at the moment.  
Example: *At 5 pm, he will be eating.*
